title: E1 Visa Lawyers | E-1 Attorneys - Colombo & Hurd
content:
## E-1 Visa Lawyers for Treaty Traders
The E-1 Treaty Trader Visa allows citizens of treaty countries to live and work in the United States by managing substantial international trade. If your country has a qualifying treaty with the U.S., this visa can open the door for you and your family to pursue new business opportunities in America.
At Colombo & Hurd, our experienced immigration attorneys have guided entrepreneurs, executives, and business owners through the E-1 visa process, helping them expand their global business presence.

## More than 20 years of Excellence in Immigration Law

## What is the E-1 Visa?
The E-1 Visa (Treaty Trader Visa) is a non-immigrant visa for citizens of countries with treaties of commerce and navigation with the United States. It enables qualified petitioners to live and work in the U.S. to manage substantial trade conducted primarily between their home country and the U.S.
Benefits of the E-1 visa:
- Visa validity depends on reciprocity of your treaty country: Each entry grants 2 years of status, regardless of visa validity
- Open to traders, executives, and key employees
- Spouse and dependent children under 21 included
- Work authorization is incident to status for E-1 spouses, meaning no separate application is required

## E-1 Visa Requirements: Do You Qualify?
To qualify, the Petitioner must be a citizen of a treaty country and demonstrate that the trading activities are significant, continuous, and conducted primarily between the U.S. and the treaty country. Employees of a qualifying organization may also be eligible if they are coming to work in an executive, supervisory, or essential skills capacity.
- Treaty Country Nationality
- Principal Trade with the U.S.
- Trade in Goods or Services
- Ownership by Treaty Nationals
- Executive, Managerial, or Essential Role
- Intent to Depart the U.S.

## Frequently Asked Questions About E-1 Visas
The U.S. State Department maintains a list of treaty countries whose nationals may apply for an E-1 Visa. While the list can be updated over time, only citizens of those designated countries are eligible.
Typically, 2 years per entry, with unlimited renewals as long as trade continues.
Yes, spouses and children under 21 can join you. Spouses may apply for U.S. work authorization.
Trade that is continuous, sizable, and creates a significant business footprint in the U.S.
While the E-1 is non-immigrant, our attorneys can advise on transitioning to permanent residency through other pathways.
